title: T-029 — MS-002 Inter-site TO Tracker dashboard (ms_ist_list) §10.1 MS-002
prompt:
# T-029 — MS-002 Inter-site TO Tracker dashboard

PRD: docs/prd/14-MULTI-SITE-PRD.md §10.1 MS-002, §10C MS-002
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/multi-site/ist-screens.jsx:6-120 (ms_ist_list)
Translation notes: _meta/prototype-labels/translation-notes-multi-site.md §ms_ist_list

## Goal
Server Component IST tracker page filtered to non-terminal states + route filter (super_admin/multi-site) + ETA coloring (red overdue, amber borderline) + state-gated row actions (view/amend/cancel) + role-gated Freight column.

## Implementation contract
1. apps/web/app/(multi-site)/transfers/page.tsx (RSC) — queries transfer_orders WHERE state NOT IN ('closed','cancelled'), joined to sites for from/to display.
2. Route filter (super_admin/multi-site only): derives from session site context — single-site users don't see filter.
3. ETA coloring via cn() utility — never inline style.
4. Linked TO/WO references render as <Link href="/planning/transfer-orders/[id]" /> or /planning/work-orders/[id].
5. Row actions gated by IST state (Server Component passes permissible actions per row).
6. Freight column hidden for warehouse_operator role (Server Component omits column).
7. Tests: RTL parity + state-gated actions + role-gated columns + ETA coloring matrix + route filter visibility.

## Files
- apps/web/app/(multi-site)/transfers/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(multi-site)/transfers/_components/ist-tracker-table.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(multi-site)/transfers/_components/__tests__/ist-tracker-table.test.tsx [create]
- prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/multi-site/ist-screens.jsx [ref]

## Acceptance criteria
<!-- AC count consolidated from 7→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost) -->
1. Given page renders, when compared to ist-screens.jsx:6-120 (ms_ist_list), then filter bar + table columns + state-machine progress badges + linked TO/WO + row actions match — RTL parity + Playwright trace.
2. Given an IST eta is past (overdue), when row renders, then the ETA cell has class 'text-red-600 font-bold' via cn() (no inline style).
3. Given user role=warehouse_operator, when page renders, then Freight column is absent (Server Component omits column).
4. Given IST state='draft', when row renders, then 'Amend' row action is shown; given state='closed' row would not be in this list (filtered out at SQL level).
5. Given super_admin user with ALL-sites context, when page renders, then route filter (from_site, to_site) is visible; single-site user does not see route filter.
6. Given page is in withSiteContext(siteId=X), when query runs, then only ISTs where from_site_id=X OR to_site_id=X are returned (RLS site-scoped).

## Test strategy
- RED: RTL parity + ETA coloring matrix + role-gated columns + state-gated actions + route filter visibility + RLS site-scoped before page exists.
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run ist-tracker-table' + Playwright ist-tracker.spec.ts.

## Prototype parity
Reference prototype: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/multi-site/ist-screens.jsx:6-120 (ms_ist_list).
Implement matching the canonical layout 1:1 per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`. Capture RTL parity snapshot + Playwright trace as closeout evidence.

## Risk red lines
- Do not paste prototype JSX wholesale.
- Do not use inline styles for ETA coloring — cn() utility required.
- Do not hide Freight column via CSS — Server Component omits column data.
- Do not include closed/cancelled ISTs — SQL-level filter.
- Do not allow row actions inconsistent with state-machine — Server Component computes per row.

Given IST eta past, when row renders, then ETA cell has class 'text-red-600 font-bold' via cn() (no inline style); AND given user role=warehouse_operator when page renders then Freight column absent (Server Component omits).

Given IST state='draft', when row renders, then 'Amend' row action shown; AND given super_admin with ALL-sites context when page renders then route filter (from_site, to_site) visible; site-scoped user sees only their site's ISTs.
Given withSiteContext(siteId=X), when query runs, then only ISTs where from_site_id=X OR to_site_id=X returned (RLS site-scoped).
